CASE DISMISSED , AS TO JULIO MESTRE BY COURT AT ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING JAN-07-2002 AT 1:30PM. THE COURT FINDS NO GOOD CAUSE OR SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE PREVIOUS COURT ORDERS. A LESS SEVERE SANCTIONS WOULD NOT BE EFFECTIVE DUE TO THE HISTORY OF LACK OF COMPLIANCE. PROCEEDINGS REPORTED BY: MARY J. HINDS. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
ORDERED TO ARBITRATION AS OF JAN-28-2002 PURSUANT TO CCP1141.11(A). NOTICE SENT BY COURT. (CONSOLID/W316662).

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-14-2002 Sent To Arbitration
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. The court having reviewed the file finds no good cause or substantial justification for plaintiff's failure to comply with the court's orders and rules. Therefore, defendant Julio Mestra is ordered dismissed without prejudice. The court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. Case is ordered to Arbitration pursuant to CCP 1141.11 (a), effective 1/28/2002. Proceedings reported by: Mary J. Hinds. (212)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2001 Continued To Jan-07-02 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: Consolidated with Case #316662 GILDE vs. CONDY. No appearance. Pltff's Csl. sanctioned $350.00, failure appear. OSC re-set to 1/7/2002 at 1:30 pm, dept. 212. Proceedings reported by: Susan Lee. (212)
